page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc8i.4xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6462C processor running at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture with a single thread per core configuration, this instance provides dedicated compute resources without hyperthreading overhead.

The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. For network connectivity, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps, facilitating reliable data transfer rates for distributed applications.

With its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ecs.hfc8i.4xlarge is structured for compute-bound workloads rather than memory-intensive or storage-heavy operations. This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for tasks requiring consistent, high-frequency CPU performance without the added expense of bundled local NVMe drives or large memory allocations. Suitable workloads include web serving, application servers, batch processing, and CI/CD pipelines.

It does not contain local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions. This configuration is designed for compute-heavy workloads such as web serving, application servers, batch processing, and CI/CD pipelines where consistent CPU performance is required and local storage is not a necessity.
